title: Isolation of SARS-Cov2 RNA from Humans Without High Demand Reagents
author: Joseph Patterson
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.be8ujhww

steps.title: Transfer the swab into a DNase/RNase free 1
steps.instruction: Transfer the swab into a DNase/RNase free 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube. •NOTE: The swabs we use can be pulled off their stick for easier processing.

steps.title: Add 500 µL of TRIzol to the microcentrifuge tube
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL of TRIzol to the microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Close tube and shake for 2 min
steps.instruction: Close tube and shake for 2 min. by hand.

steps.title: Remove the swab and briefly centrifuge. •NOTE
steps.instruction: Remove the swab and briefly centrifuge. •NOTE: The swab should be removed from the TRIzol, and squeezed against the side of the tube to retain as much sample as possible.

steps.title: Add 100 uL of 100% chloroform to the phasemaker tube
steps.instruction: Add 100 uL of 100% chloroform to the phasemaker tube.

steps.title: Shake the tube for 15 seconds...
steps.instruction: Shake the tube for 15 seconds by hand (DO NOT VORTEX).

steps.title: Incubate for 10 min
steps.instruction: Incubate for 10 min. at room temperature.

steps.title: Centrifuge for 5 min
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 5 min. at 16,000xg at 4°C.

steps.title: Transfer the aqueous phase (clear) to...
steps.instruction: Transfer the aqueous phase (clear) to a new DNase/RNase free 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube, and dispose of the Phasemaker tube.

steps.title: Add 250 µL of 100% isopropanol
steps.instruction: Add 250 µL of 100% isopropanol

steps.title: Add 2 µL of GlycoBlue coprecipitant
steps.instruction: Add 2 µL of GlycoBlue coprecipitant

steps.title: Vortex for 30 s
steps.instruction: Vortex for 30 s.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 12,000 x g for 20 min
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 12,000 x g for 20 min. at 4 ºC.

steps.title: Discard the supernatant, keep the pellet
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant, keep the pellet.

steps.title: Add 80% ethanol to wash the pellet
steps.instruction: Add 80% ethanol to wash the pellet.

steps.title: Immediately remove all of the ethanol. •NOTE
steps.instruction: Immediately remove all of the ethanol. •NOTE: Once most of the ethanol is removed, the tubes can be briefly centrifuged and a pipet used to remove as much of the remaining ethanol as possible.

steps.title: Allow pellet to dry for 3 min
steps.instruction: Allow pellet to dry for 3 min. at room temperature.

steps.title: Resuspend pellet in nuclease free water
steps.instruction: Resuspend pellet in nuclease free water.

steps.title: Centrifuge Phasemaker tubes (Invitrogen A33248) for 30 s at 16,000xg
steps.instruction: Centrifuge Phasemaker tubes (Invitrogen A33248) for 30 s at 16,000xg

steps.title: Transfer the entire sample to a...
steps.instruction: Transfer the entire sample to a Phasemaker tube and incubate at RT for 5 min

steps.title: If Phasemaker tubes are not available,...
steps.instruction: If Phasemaker tubes are not available, substitute the steps below for steps 6-11: 1)Add 100 uL of 100% chloroform. 2)Vortex for 30 s. 3)Incubate for 3 min. at room temperature. 4)Centrifuge at 12,000 x g for 10 min. at 4 ºC. 5)Transfer supernatant to a new DNase/RNase free 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube. 6) Continue with protocol at step 7, adding 250 µL of 100% isopropanol to each tube.

steps.title: Samples can be frozen at -80...
steps.instruction: Samples can be frozen at -80 ºC at this point if needed, then thawed for use when ready.

steps.title: For both droplet digital PCR and...
steps.instruction: For both droplet digital PCR and qPCR protocols, 25µL nuclease free water is an appropriate starting volume, but can be adjusted if needed.
